jokes.text: They take no prisoners... because they are prisoners... at the Orange County Correctional Facility L.I.T.E.
jokes.setup: Narrator describes a gang that 'takes no prisoners' - sounds threatening
jokes.punchline: Reveals they ARE prisoners themselves, subverting expectations
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect economy. The pause/repeat structure builds tension then releases it perfectly.

jokes.text: They're the ruling gang in this four-star hellhole
jokes.setup: Prison described as 'four-star'
jokes.punchline: Immediately called a 'hellhole' - contradictory descriptions
jokes.explanation: Solid ironic contradiction but fairly predictable prison humor.

jokes.text: Real Asian Prison Housewives of the Orange County White Collar Prison System
jokes.setup: Parody of Real Housewives reality TV format
jokes.punchline: Applied to Asian prison gang members
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture parody that fits AD's documentary mockumentary style well.

jokes.text: one of our members is a real 'B' word
jokes.setup: Tough gang member about to use harsh language
jokes.punchline: Euphemistically calls someone the 'B word' instead of saying 'bitch'
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing even tough gang members are oddly polite on camera.

jokes.text: In civilian life, we'd just vote Lu out, but here... she's gonna end up on the wrong end of a noodle
jokes.setup: Comparing gang justice to reality TV voting
jokes.punchline: Threatening murder with 'wrong end of a noodle'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of reality TV democracy with prison violence, delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Uncooked. You get me?
jokes.setup: Previous noodle threat
jokes.punchline: Clarification that the noodle weapon is uncooked makes it more deadly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd noodle weapon concept with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Their bark is louder than the dogs they eat back home
jokes.setup: Variation on 'bark is worse than bite' saying
jokes.punchline: Lucille's racist assumption about Asian cuisine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille character moment - casually racist but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: Anyone who comes after me is going to be one unfortunate cookie
jokes.setup: Lucille making threats back at the gang
jokes.punchline: Uses 'unfortunate cookie' as a threat (fortune cookie reference)
jokes.explanation: Lucille's attempt at Asian-themed intimidation shows her oblivious racism.

jokes.text: I made a pun
jokes.setup: Lucille just delivered the 'unfortunate cookie' line
jokes.punchline: She proudly announces she made a pun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - she's so pleased with her weak wordplay she has to announce it.

jokes.text: It's Lucille's 'Arrested Development'
jokes.setup: Standard title card format
jokes.punchline: Show title becomes about Lucille's specific situation
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment playing with the show's own title.

jokes.text: Years before her behavior had become 'bossy, bossy' with the Jade Dragon Triad
jokes.setup: Reference to earlier gang member complaint
jokes.punchline: Calling Lucille 'bossy, bossy' in narrator voice
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that connects timeline, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Full steam, boys!
jokes.setup: Lucille fleeing the SEC on a boat
jokes.punchline: She's giving nautical commands like a ship captain
jokes.explanation: Lucille taking charge with old-timey naval commands is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: you're usually as hard to miss as Liberace wearing a fright wig
jokes.setup: Lucille claims she can't see Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing her to flamboyant Liberace
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted insult that sounds like a compliment - classic Lucille passive-aggression.

jokes.text: I'm practically counting your crow's feet
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 responding to Lucille's insults
jokes.punchline: Insults Lucille's wrinkles while pretending to be friendly
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the passive-aggressive battle between the Lucilles.

jokes.text: maybe I should turn back and show you another bird
jokes.setup: Lucille wants to give Lucille 2 the finger
jokes.punchline: Euphemistically calls it 'another bird'
jokes.explanation: Lucille's attempt at crude humor while maintaining her facade of propriety.

jokes.text: But it was clear that Lucille 2 hadn't heard it
jokes.setup: Lucille's proud delivery of her 'bird' insult
jokes.punchline: Nobody heard her clever comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Lucille's moment of satisfaction - classic AD anticlimax.

jokes.text: in a fit of what sailors call 'sea rage'
jokes.setup: Lucille getting angry on the water
jokes.punchline: Narrator makes up 'sea rage' as if it's a real nautical term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake nautical terminology delivered with complete authority.

jokes.text: Coming about, boys!
jokes.setup: Lucille continuing her ship captain act
jokes.punchline: More earnest nautical commands in her rage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Lucille's nautical delusions of grandeur.

jokes.text: in what can only be described as a 'sail-past' gone wrong
jokes.setup: Boat crash described in formal language
jokes.punchline: 'Sail-past' is a pun on 'drive-by' but with sailing
jokes.explanation: Clever nautical wordplay that makes a boat crash sound like a failed military maneuver.

jokes.text: Correction: good news for Barry
jokes.setup: Barry promises good news about legal situation
jokes.punchline: Admits the 'good news' is only good for him (more fees)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment - accidentally revealing his self-interest while trying to help.

jokes.text: She stole the 'Queen Mary' to get away from the SEC. Oh, and she winked.
jokes.setup: Buster supposed to provide alibi for Lucille
jokes.punchline: Immediately tells the truth, then mentions her wink
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster - completely misses the point and sabotages the plan instantly.

jokes.text: Correction: it's a glare
jokes.setup: Buster mentions Lucille winked
jokes.punchline: Lucille corrects that it was a glare, not a wink
jokes.explanation: Lucille's prideful correction shows she can't help but maintain her intimidating image.

jokes.text: It's only a trial separation. I made a pun.
jokes.setup: George Sr. talking about their fake divorce
jokes.punchline: Realizes 'trial separation' is a pun given Lucille's trial
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Lucille's pun announcement, now George Sr. does it too.

jokes.text: Even in this time of stress
jokes.setup: George Sr. just made a pun about their serious situation
jokes.punchline: He's proud of making a pun during a crisis
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s misplaced pride in his wordplay mirrors Lucille perfectly.

jokes.text: This toast has hook holes... again! I told you to get rid of that thing; it's hideous.
jokes.setup: Buster's hook is causing practical problems
jokes.punchline: His hook is poking holes in toast, Lucille finds it hideous
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy and perfectly captures Lucille's vanity over practicality.

jokes.text: what's gonna happen when they swear you in? You're gonna rip the Bible in half. The jury will love that.
jokes.setup: Lucille worried about Buster's hook in court
jokes.punchline: Imagines him destroying the Bible during swearing-in
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the hook problem to courtroom disaster with perfect sarcasm.

jokes.text: Maybe you could hook me up with a little stimmy. Lil' stimmy.
jokes.setup: Buster asking for something from Lucille
jokes.punchline: Uses 'hook me up' literally since he has a hook, wants stimulus
jokes.explanation: Great Buster wordplay combining his hook with his need for stimulation/drugs.

jokes.text: And I want stimmy to get a hook that'll do that
jokes.setup: Buster wants stimulation
jokes.punchline: He wants a special hook for the stimulation gesture
jokes.explanation: Buster's innocent request for a masturbation-specific hook attachment is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Homosexuals kidnapped the boat and took it on a joy ride. Mother didn't want to trample their rights, so she let them.
jokes.setup: Buster rehearsing his court testimony
jokes.punchline: Absurd story about gay boat thieves and Lucille's liberal tolerance
jokes.explanation: Hilariously absurd testimony that makes Lucille sound like an unlikely civil rights champion.

jokes.text: Make it an eel. It's gonna be an eel! Better.
jokes.setup: Buster mentions a fish swam by his ankle
jokes.punchline: Lucille upgrades it to an eel for more dramatic effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Lucille's dramatization instincts - even marine life needs to be more threatening.

jokes.text: I'm staring at a hundred years in the face! How dare you.
jokes.setup: Buster says he's staring five years in the face
jokes.punchline: Lucille thinks he's calling her a hundred years old
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection where Lucille's vanity makes her misinterpret prison time as an age insult.

jokes.text: It sure would be a shame if something happened to your pretty little key witness. Star witness. And are you threatening yourself?
jokes.setup: Lucille threatening Buster in third person
jokes.punchline: Buster points out she's essentially threatening herself
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment where the threat structure collapses under its own logic.

jokes.text: And he's supposed to be the good one
jokes.setup: Lucille discovers family has divided up her possessions
jokes.punchline: Michael, supposedly the moral one, is also claiming her stuff
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille disappointment in Michael's moral failings, delivered with wounded dignity.

jokes.text: Actually, they'd claimed those things years earlier when she was having a sunspot checked
jokes.setup: Lucille thinks family is preparing for her death
jokes.punchline: They actually did this during a routine medical procedure years ago
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and perfect example of the family's opportunistic nature.

jokes.text: I've made a pun. Oh, how could I not do it for my oldest friend? Oh! Now I've gone and done a double. A double.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 calls Lucille a 'large shareholder'
jokes.punchline: She announces making a pun, then realizes 'oldest friend' is another pun
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the pun-announcing running gag, now with double puns.

jokes.text: Like your pre-surgery chin. Or your post-breakfast drink.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 mentions 'double'
jokes.punchline: Lucille fires back with double-entendre about drinking and plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Devastating comeback that perfectly captures their competitive cruelty - economical and brutal.

jokes.text: Your references are as fresh as the wallpaper in the room of that pretend child you've had all these years
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 responding to Lucille's attacks
jokes.punchline: References Buster as a 'pretend child' implying he's emotionally stunted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult that cuts deep about both outdated references and Buster's arrested development.

jokes.text: Your pillow must look like a Rorschach test. Not that there'd be anyone to see it.
jokes.setup: Lucille attacking Lucille 2's appearance
jokes.punchline: Implies her makeup stains pillows, then adds she's alone anyway
jokes.explanation: Devastating two-part insult that starts visual then becomes deeply personal - masterful escalation.

jokes.text: Says the woman whose liver can be spotted from outer space
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 mentions Lucille's 'liver-spotted claws'
jokes.punchline: Lucille counters that Lucille 2's liver damage is visible from space
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the liver spot comment with cosmic-level exaggeration.

jokes.text: Well, our cashier's pregnant, and she's not married, so...
jokes.setup: Lucille asks if clerk has heard anything about her case
jokes.punchline: Clerk shares irrelevant prison gossip instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing the clerk's priority on gossip over Lucille's legal fate.

jokes.text: A $20 gratuity is appreciated?
jokes.setup: Guard explaining what to do if sexually assaulted
jokes.punchline: Lucille thinks she's supposed to tip her attacker
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Lucille - she interprets assault protocol as service industry etiquette.

jokes.text: The same girl who hands me towels does the cavity checks. You tip.
jokes.setup: Lucille explaining prison spa amenities
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of tipping for invasive body searches
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Lucille treats degrading prison procedures like luxury spa services.

jokes.text: That's a gang? I thought they were tech support.
jokes.setup: George Sr. tells Lucille about the Jade Triad Gang
jokes.punchline: Lucille thought Asian prisoners were tech support
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille racism delivered with casual ignorance - perfectly in character.

jokes.text: 'Roop hore'? Roop hore. You couldn't have chosen a harder word to say.
jokes.setup: Lucille explaining a 'loophole' to Asian gang members
jokes.punchline: They can't pronounce 'loophole' due to accent stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Lazy ethnic humor relying on pronunciation stereotypes rather than character or situation

jokes.text: Your move, Olive Garden
jokes.setup: Lucille playing mahjong with China Garden
jokes.punchline: Calls her 'Olive Garden' like the Italian restaurant chain
jokes.explanation: Lucille's confusion about ethnic names creates accidental restaurant reference.

jokes.text: You couldn't have chosen a harder word to say
jokes.setup: Lucille trying to say 'loophole' to Chinese gang members
jokes.punchline: Narrator points out it's the worst word for them to pronounce
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta commentary on the linguistic challenge Lucille accidentally created.

jokes.text: And soon the Dragons were taking 'drags on' their cigarettes again
jokes.setup: The Jade Dragons getting cigarettes through Lucille's loophole
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Dragons' and 'drags' (cigarette puffs)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that connects the gang name to smoking behavior.

jokes.text: I got Olive Garden to offer us unlimited bread
jokes.setup: George Sr. negotiating with the Chinese for wall funding
jokes.punchline: Still calling China Garden 'Olive Garden' and mentions unlimited breadsticks
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the restaurant confusion to their business deal.

jokes.text: Who better than the Chinese to help us build a wall, right?
jokes.setup: George Sr. explaining the business partnership
jokes.punchline: References Great Wall of China while building U.S.-Mexico border wall
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about Chinese wall-building expertise being used against Mexico.

jokes.text: Ancient Chinese secret, huh? It's no secret-- we'll kill you.
jokes.setup: Lucille responds to death threat with old commercial reference
jokes.punchline: Gang member deadpans that murder isn't actually a secret technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that deflates Lucille's casual racism with brutal honesty.

jokes.text: My God, he Steamboat Willied it
jokes.setup: George Sr. sends spinning/looping video evidence of wall construction
jokes.punchline: Lucille references old Mickey Mouse cartoon with repetitive animation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reference to early animation techniques to describe modern video manipulation.

jokes.text: I can't tell you who, 'cause I'm dating someone famous. someone... famous.
jokes.setup: Gob explains why he can't reveal who called
jokes.punchline: Awkward repetition suggests he's lying about dating someone famous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - his lie breaks down under the weight of his own insecurity.

jokes.text: That was an a...?
jokes.setup: Lucille says she'll put on 'the old drunk act'
jokes.punchline: Gob realizes her drinking might not have been an act
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment where Gob questions his mother's sobriety history.

jokes.text: I'm as normal as blueberry pie
jokes.setup: Lucille trying to convince Tobias she's mentally healthy
jokes.punchline: Uses bizarre comparison that actually sounds abnormal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - her attempt at folksy normalcy reveals how out of touch she is.

jokes.text: You look as swishy as Ryan Seacrest. Ryan Seacrest is straight. And I'm 40!
jokes.setup: Lucille insulting Tobias's appearance
jokes.punchline: Her defense of Ryan Seacrest and age lie reveal her delusions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Lucille's vanity and pop culture confusion.

jokes.text: Perhaps that's why they call this place Au-stare-ity
jokes.setup: Lucille and Tobias staring at each other in silence
jokes.punchline: Tobias makes terrible pun on 'Austerity' and 'stare'
jokes.explanation: Classic bad Tobias pun that he's proud of despite it being terrible.

jokes.text: He thinks that horrible monster looks like me! So fine, you want to call me a villain, I accept it! I'm your villain!
jokes.setup: Lucille's emotional breakdown about being seen as a villain
jokes.punchline: She dramatically embraces the villain role
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Lucille's dramatic self-pity turning into theatrical acceptance

jokes.text: Take to the sea!
jokes.setup: Tobias mentions his musical will be performed on a boat
jokes.punchline: Lucille's instinctive response to any boat-related opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Lucille's consistent escape plan - boats are always her solution.

jokes.text: ♪ My heart is in pain / I just want to flee / From me. ♪
jokes.setup: Lucille singing audition song
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly honest and vulnerable lyrics ending in self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character moment - rare genuine vulnerability from Lucille with clever wordplay

jokes.text: Rules are meant to be broken, huh? We have Dove bars?
jokes.setup: Michael offering ice cream bribe to child
jokes.punchline: Kid more excited about the ice cream existing than the rule-breaking
jokes.explanation: Good child character moment - priorities completely different from adult expectations.

jokes.text: Gene! He does this to me every time! What's he doing here, Mom? I thought he was that guy.
jokes.setup: Gene Parmesan appears in disguise
jokes.punchline: Michael startled but Lucille explains she hired him, thought he was someone else
jokes.explanation: Classic Gene Parmesan running gag with typical family confusion.

jokes.text: She missed a step. Tell her she missed a step. She can't do it like that.
jokes.setup: DeBrie struggling with dance rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Lucille giving director-like commands despite being a replacement cast member
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - immediately takes control and criticizes everyone despite being new.

jokes.text: Do you think I'm here for the donkey punch? Which I do love, and it's the only night you can get it.
jokes.setup: Oscar declaring his love for Lucille
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of violent sexual practice at Cinco celebration
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Oscar oversharing that perfectly captures his inappropriate honesty.

jokes.text: What you are is the Invisible Girl. You make it impossible for people to see you in order to protect yourself.
jokes.setup: Tobias doing therapy breakthrough with Lucille
jokes.punchline: Connects her psychology to Fantastic Four character powers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moment where Tobias's comic book obsession actually provides genuine psychological insight.

jokes.text: They're going to be talking about this bomb for years
jokes.setup: Tobias on boat after his musical performance
jokes.punchline: Uses 'bomb' to mean failed performance, not knowing there's a literal bomb
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Tobias unknowingly predicts the actual bomb threat with theater terminology.

jokes.text: They're going to be talking about this bomb for years. Oh, God, it is bad, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Tobias reflecting on his musical performance
jokes.punchline: Double meaning - 'bomb' as failure vs. actual explosive device
jokes.explanation: Perfect wordplay misdirection that works on multiple levels - theatrical failure vs. literal bomb
